title: Verilog reader fails on nested generates that each declare wire with same name
body:
# Repro instructions

test4.v:
```Verilog
module test4 (data_in);

input wire [127:0] data_in;

genvar l0;
genvar l1;

generate
for(l0=0;l0<4;l0=l0+1)
  begin : g0
    wire [31:0] data = data_in[63:32];

    for(l1=0;l1<4;l1=l1+1)
      begin : g1
        wire [7:0] data = data_in[63:56];
      end

  end
endgenerate

endmodule
```

Execute:

    yosys -p "read_verilog -sv test4.v"

# Actual Result

```
 Yosys 0.9+932 (git sha1 0d037bf, gcc 5.4.0-6ubuntu1~16.04.11 -Og -fPIC)


-- Running command `read_verilog -sv test4.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: test4.v
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `test4.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\test4'.
test4.v:11: ERROR: Failed to detect width of signal access `\g0[0].data'!
```

# Expected Result

No error.

It works fine on iverilog, verilator, and a bunch of commercial tools.
code_blocks:
Yosys 0.9+932 (git sha1 0d037bf, gcc 5.4.0-6ubuntu1~16.04.11 -Og -fPIC)


-- Running command `read_verilog -sv test4.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: test4.v
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `test4.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\test4'.
test4.v:11: ERROR: Failed to detect width of signal access `\g0[0].data'!
